page: Microsoft Azure F8s is an FS family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor with an x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz, offering 8 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. The processor includes a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, with hardware support for AVX, AVX-512F, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instructions.

The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This allocation aligns with compute-optimized hardware profiles. Local storage is limited to a 33 GB volume, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. It does not include any bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance split between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression tasks like gzip and bzip3. Conversely, multi-core performance is average, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth, static web serving throughput, and Redis SET operations also perform within the average tier.

Qualitatively, the F8s offers a balanced cost-to-performance ratio for multi-threaded workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent execution, though the low single-core performance presents a bottleneck for single-threaded tasks. This server is suitable for multi-threaded software compilation, image processing, ray tracing, and moderate database operations.
description: Microsoft Azure F8s is an FS family virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8171M processor operating at 2.6 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB of memory per core. The instance is Premium Storage capable and includes a 33 GB local storage volume, with no bundled GPUs. Performance benchmarks show a distinct tradeoff: single-core metrics, such as Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression, fall into the bottom 25%, while multi-core metrics, including stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU tests, rank in the middle 50%. Memory bandwidth, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also perform at average levels. This resource profile is suitable for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, image processing, and moderate database operations that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation but do not require high single-core speeds.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F8s is an FS family virtual machine with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 33 GB storage, delivering average multi-core and weak single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F8s is an 8-vCPU virtual machine with 16 GB RAM and 33 GB storage, suitable for multi-threaded workloads and software compilation.
tagline: A dedicated eight-core compute-optimized virtual machine featuring balanced multi-core performance and premium storage capabilities for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8171M vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per core)
33 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average multi-core performance but weak single-core execution
Suited for software compilation, image processing, and database operations
